name: Algebra and Equations
description: Linear and quadratic equations, systems of equations, inequalities, algebraic expressions and factorization.
label: ALG

content.text: Algebra is a branch of mathematics that deals with symbolic computation and solving equations. It works with variables, constants, and operations to express general mathematical relationships.

A linear equation has the form ax + b = 0, where a and b are constants. It is solved by isolating the variable x. The graph of a linear function is a straight line. Systems of linear equations can be solved by substitution, elimination, or graphical methods.

A quadratic equation has the form ax² + bx + c = 0. It is solved using the discriminant D = b² - 4ac. If D > 0, the equation has two real roots; if D = 0, it has one repeated root; if D < 0, it has no real solution.

Factorization is the decomposition of an algebraic expression into a product of simpler expressions. Key techniques include factoring out common factors, decomposing quadratic trinomials, and using sum and difference of powers formulas.
